nodes.context: Italian-American gang in New York City involved in heroin trafficking, murder, and contract killing during the 1970s-1980s.
One of the Five Families of New York; the Purple Gang was originally affiliated with them.
One of the Five Families; the Purple Gang worked with them and later had rivalry with Carmine Galante's faction.
One of the Five Families; the Purple Gang developed closest ties with them and remnants joined their 116th Street Crew.
Crew of the Genovese crime family that absorbed the remnants of the Purple Gang.
Prohibition-era gang in Detroit that the East Harlem Purple Gang named themselves after.
Allied organized crime group mentioned in the infobox as an ally of the Purple Gang.
Allied organized crime group mentioned in the infobox as an ally of the Purple Gang.
Cuban organized crime group in Florida suspected of ties with the Purple Gang.

edges.context: The Purple Gang was originally affiliated with and worked with the Lucchese crime family.
The Purple Gang worked with the Bonanno crime family, but later had rivalry with Carmine Galante's faction.
The Purple Gang developed its closest ties with the Genovese family and remnants joined their 116th Street Crew.
The Purple Gang was absorbed into the Genovese family's 116th Street Crew.
The East Harlem Purple Gang named themselves after the Prohibition-era Detroit Purple Gang.
The Council is listed as an ally in the infobox.
The Corporation is listed as an ally in the infobox.
The Purple Gang was suspected of having ties to the Cuban mafia in Florida.
Law enforcement feared a mob war between the Purple Gang and Carmine Galante's Bonanno family.
